response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with a strong web/cloud software background, but the role requires deep hardware-level expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance architecture, and deep-learning workload analysis at the silicon level. Nothing in his 17-year career touches SIMD/MIMD architectures, GPU workload profiling, or hardware-software co-design for AI chips. His closest relevant experience is low-latency async I/O work at Dunnhumby and C/C++ tooling at National Instruments, but neither approaches the hardware architecture depth this principal-level role demands.
strengths: Long career (~17 years) demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and adaptability across domains
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, fibers, real-time metadata) shows performance engineering awareness
C and C++ experience at National Instruments provides some hardware-adjacent tooling context
concerns: Zero SoC/IP modeling experience — the core technical requirement of the role is entirely absent from the resume
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization at any level, hardware or software
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator experience; entire career is web, mobile, and backend software

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture background
No AI/deep-learning workload or accelerator experience
